Yine, Meclis personeli arasında maaş uygulamasının adil bir hâle getirilmesi gerektiği... Yine, daha önce de ifade etmiştik, bu konuda bir düzenleme oldu ama yeterli değil, vejetaryen ve vegan arkadaşlar için menü alternatifleri artırılmalı. Yine, güvenlik meselesinde, girişte keyfî uygulamalar olduğu meselesi bize çok ifade edildi. Bu doğrultuda zaman zaman yaşanan zorlayıcı yaklaşımlardan bahsedildi. Kapıdaki polisler tarafından kişilere sert ve olağan şüpheliymiş yaklaşımıyla yaklaşıldığı
ifade edildi.
Bir mesele daha... Güvenlik birimine ait bir genelge olduğu ifade ediliyor ama bu genelgeye nedense ben ulaşamıyorum. Bu genelge bir devlet sırrı mı, niye bu devlet sırrı gibi saklanıyor, ben niye ulaşamıyorum? Buradan ifade etmek istiyorum.
Yine, Meclisteki bahçede ve çevresinde hayvanlar için beslenme ve yaşam koşulları geliştirilmeli yani aslında Meclis ekolojik yaşam için de bütçe ayırmalı.
Yine, bir diğer konu da Meclis çatısı altında görev yapan danışmanların, sekreterlerin, emekçilerin hakları hâlâ sürüncemede. Bu konuyu defalarca dile de getirdik ama danışman arkadaşlara ilişkin henüz bir düzenleme olmadı. Evet, bir tekliften, bir düzenlemeden bahsediliyor ama defalardır bu meseleye dikkat çekiyoruz ama hâlâ bu konuyla ilgili bir düzenleme olmadı. Hak tartışmalarının merkezinde neden bu arkadaşların hakları hâlâ görmezden geliniyor? Aynı işi yapan kadrolu ve sözleşmeli danışmanlar arasında uçurum var. Promosyon ödemeleri şeffaflıkla yürütülmeli; giyim ve yol hakkına, yemekhane ücretlerinden yeşil pasaport hakkına kadar tekrar bunları konuşmak zorundayız. Bunları tekrar tekrar konuşmak gerçekten bu Meclise uymuyor. Yine, bu arkadaşlara da yeşil pasaportun verilmesi meselesini de gündemimize almamız gerekiyor. Buradan bir kez daha şunu ifade etmek istiyoruz: Bu, arkadaşların anayasal haklarıdır. Meclisi de ayakta tutan bu emekçiler için hepimizin bu çalışmayı bir an önce yapması gerekiyor.
Süre bitti mi?
BAŞKAN MEHMET MUŞ - Yok, devam edin.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Pardon, bir anda sesimi duymayınca...
Evet, bu, Meclisteki personellerin, emekçilerin onuruna vurulmuş bir durumdur. Bu konuda gerçekten üzerimize düşeni yapmamız gerektiğini bir kez daha ifade ediyorum.
Önümüzdeki süreçte bu Mecliste gerçekten önemli işler de yapıldı. İşte, Millî Dayanışma, Kardeşlik ve Demokrasi Komisyonunun bu Meclis çatısı altında kurulmasını ve gerçekten bu ülkenin barışının bu Meclis çatısı altında konuşulmasını çok önemsiyoruz ve barış için emeğini ortaya koyan, barış için bu çalışmada yer alan, barış için elinden geleni yapan herkese de teşekkür ediyoruz.
Ben teşekkür ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Thank you, President.
President, dear MPs; Yes, when we talk about the budget process here today, I think we should feel the weight of the economic depression and economic crisis that millions of people in this country are experiencing on our shoulders, but when we look at the budget preparation this year, as every time, unfortunately, women, youth and laborers are not included in this budget. Unfortunately, we cannot talk about a real budget that is suitable for the difficult conditions we are in and that will give the society a breath of fresh air. Everyone needs to take responsibility for a budget that can bring hope to this country, we have a historical responsibility. We emphasize that this year's budget should be approached with this historical responsibility, but unfortunately, this is not what we see here today. Unfortunately, every budget prepared under the shadow of a centralist, monist and unequal order deepens injustice, ignores different identities, cultures and languages, and reproduces poverty and discrimination.
Again, the budget is no longer a document negotiated in the Parliament, but has turned into a text, a document created unilaterally by the executive branch. The work of the Planning and Budget Commission remains for show. The opinions of unions, professional organizations, democratic mass organizations and local governments are not reflected in this budget, the people's voice is not reflected in the people's budget, the right to the budget has been taken away from the people; Unfortunately, this is what we see today.
Another violation of rights and intolerance experienced in the Parliament is discrimination and intolerance towards the languages ​​that have lived in this geography for thousands of years. We saw this very clearly in the Parliament during this period, Kurdish, the mother tongue of millions of citizens, is still ignored under this umbrella and written as an "unknown language".
Every Kurdish word spoken in the parliamentary rostrum is an unknown language in the minutes. Or the microphones were turned off one by one. This is not acceptable. Kurdish is not spoken even in the Commission established for peace and brotherhood. Indeed, the first step to be taken for a democratic solution to the Kurdish problem should be to make peace with the Kurdish language. In the 21st century, the excuse that we do not understand or do not know the native language is unacceptable, we do not find this correct. Each of us should be able to learn the language of the people we live with, we should learn it or have a translator. I think we should put the issue of having a translator in the Parliament on our agenda in the coming period. The opposite is denial, assimilation, and stands before us as the biggest obstacle to equal citizenship and social peace.
Again, another issue where pluralism is ignored is the reality of law making, unfortunately, by the Parliament. In the past period, dozens of laws and omnibus bills consisting of dozens of articles were passed by the Parliament, and it was as if the problems of every ministry and every field were superficially put into a bag and turned into a matter of extracting dozens of demons from each bag. The legislative process was essentially manipulated in this way. For example, the 80-article law adopted in the General Assembly on June 22, 2025 was an omnibus law and the problems of many ministries were in it. It was a bill that brought together many different areas such as the Olive Farming Protection Law, Pasture Law, Forest Law and Environmental Law.

This form of legislation ignores the principle of justice and representation. This seriously undermines the credibility and weight of the Parliament in the eyes of the public. Democracy is not a show of pulling rabbits out of a hat; It is the main responsibility of the Parliament to operate with transparency and social negotiation. I would like to express that it is necessary to pay attention to this in this process.
Dear friends, this Parliament should be the first source of pluralism, labor and justice. In order for democracy to truly function, the rights and honor of not only the MPs, but also the consultants, secretaries, cafeteria workers, teahouse workers and cleaning workers must be protected under this roof. But unfortunately, the inequalities and discrimination faced by those working in this Parliament still continue. We have received dozens of complaints about this issue. For example, personnel vehicles, the application of barriers to personnel vehicles, this issue of barrier application should be abolished. Again, sufficient parking space should be provided and old parking lots should be opened. Again, access should be facilitated at the door leading to the hospital. Many working friends have to walk from here to the hospital. The practice of body-searching personnel passing through Dikmen Gate should be stopped immediately. The issue of employees not being able to enter and exit from every door is really not an acceptable issue. Again, the pedagogical approach should be taken as the basis in the nursery. Impositions under the name of "values" should be abandoned. Padlocks are being installed in playgrounds, this should be stopped. Again, nurseries and classes for younger age groups should be opened. We really need to put on our agenda the issue of expanding research, study and reading areas in the library - yes, the President also stated it. Again, merit should be taken into account when recruiting personnel. We received many complaints about quality and hygiene in the staff cafeteria. We need to do our part in this regard. Again, the salary practice among Parliament staff should be made fair. Again, as we have stated before, there has been a regulation on this issue, but it is not enough, menu alternatives for vegetarian and vegan friends should be increased. Again, regarding the security issue, it has been frequently expressed to us that there are arbitrary practices at the entrance. In this regard, challenging approaches from time to time were mentioned. People were approached harshly and as if they were ordinary suspects by the police at the door.
was expressed.
One more thing. It is stated that there is a circular from the security unit, but for some reason I cannot access this circular. Is this circular a state secret? Why is it kept as a state secret? Why can't I access it? I want to express it here.
Again, feeding and living conditions for animals in and around the garden in the Parliament should be improved, that is, the Parliament should also allocate a budget for ecological life.
Another issue is that the rights of consultants, secretaries and workers working under the umbrella of the Parliament are still pending. We have raised this issue many times, but there has been no regulation regarding consultant friends yet. Yes, there is talk of a proposal, a regulation, but we have been drawing attention to this issue many times, but there is still no regulation on this issue.

Why are the rights of these friends still ignored at the center of rights discussions? There is a gap between permanent and contracted consultants doing the same job. Promotional payments should be conducted with transparency; We have to talk about these again, from clothing and road rights to cafeteria fees and green passport rights. Talking about these things over and over again really does not suit this Parliament. Again, we need to put the issue of giving green passports to these friends on our agenda. We would like to express this once again: This is the constitutional rights of friends. We all need to do this work as soon as possible for these workers who keep the parliament afloat.
Is time over?
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - No, continue.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Sorry, when you suddenly didn't hear my voice.
Yes, this is a violation of the honor of the staff and workers in the Parliament. I express once again that we really need to do our part in this regard.
Really important work has been done in this Parliament in the coming period. We attach great importance to the establishment of the National Solidarity, Fraternity and Democracy Commission under the roof of this Parliament and to discuss the peace of this country under the roof of this Parliament, and we thank everyone who puts forth their efforts for peace, takes part in this work for peace, and does their best for peace.
I thank you.

records.originalTurkish:
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Teşekkürler Başkan.
Ben de herkesi saygıyla selamlıyorum.
Öncelikle artık son bulması için mücadele yürüttüğümüz savaş politikaları sonucunda yirmi bir yıl önce Kızıltepe'de katledilen Uğur Kaymaz'ın bugün ölüm yıl dönümü, saygıyla anarak başlamak istiyorum.
Evet, sağlıkta çok güzel bir tablo çizdi Sayın Bakan ama öncelikle Sağlıkta Dönüşüm Programı'na bile baktığımızda bu tablonun böyle olmadığı gerçekten gözler önüne seriliyor. Derinleşen yolsuzluk, Sağlıkta Dönüşüm Programı'nda kurumsal yetersizlikler, liyakatsiz atamalar, paran kadar sağlık durumu, şehir hastaneleri marifetiyle özel hastanelere, özel sermayeye aktarılan milyonlar. Yine sistematik olarak emeğin güvencesizliği, sağlıkta çeteleşme, sağlık çalışanlarının karşılaşmış olduğu şiddet vakaları ve bölgesel eşitsizlikler maalesef derinleşti. Sonuçta hastanın, vatandaşın değil; ihalenin, sermayedarın; sağlık çalışanının değil, şirketlerin önceliği var bu sistemde. Sistemin şah damarı artık rant, denetimsizlik ve güvencesizlikle tıkanmış durumda. Yirmi yıldır "dönüşüm" diyorsunuz, "reform" diyorsunuz ancak hasta müşteriye, hastaneler AVM'ye, hekimlik performansa, sağlık çalışanları da taşerona dönüştü maalesef. 2003 yılında IMF ve Dünya Bankası eliyle Türkiye'ye giydirilmiş ve etiketi düşmüş bir eski gömlektir Sağlıkta Dönüşüm ama biz bu gömleğe sığmadık maalesef. Genel sağlık sigortası kapsayıcılığıyla böyle bir vaatle başladı ancak milyonlarca yurttaşın borç yüküyle karşı karşıya kaldı. Şehir hastaneleri şehir dışlarına yapıldı. 5 hastaneye ödenen kira Sağlık Bakanlığının yatırım bütçesini de geçmiş durumda. Bu hastaneler açılırken kamu hastaneleri kapatıldı maalesef. Ulaşım zorlaştı, hizmet azaldı, iş yükü arttı, yolsuzluklar meşrulaştırıldı bu sistemle. Bebek ve çocuk ölümleri dahi şirketlerin kazançlarına endekslendi. Bakanlık da ölüm karşısında sadece para cezalarıyla yetindi.
Yine, sağlıktaki en çok sorunlardan biri de ana dilinde sağlık hizmetinin olmayışı. Türkiye'de milyonlarca Kürt var ve bunların ana dili de Kürtçedir. Ana dilinde hizmet maalesef yok. Milyonlarca kişinin dilinin yasak olması, yüzlerce, milyonlarca hasta ile sağlık çalışanları arasında iletişim sorunları yaşanmasına sebep oldu ve bunlar da birçok hayati meseleye sebep oldu. Bununla ilgili onlarca, yüzlerce örnek verebiliriz. Bu sadece bir iletişim sorunu değil tabii ki, doğrudan tıbbi bir hata ve ölüm nedenidir. Dünya Sağlık Örgütünün ana dilinde sağlık hizmeti standardı maalesef Türkiye'de kâğıt üzerinde bile yok. O yüzden ana dilinde sağlık hizmeti aslında olmazsa olmaz bir hizmet olmalı.
Yine, Mardin'e, vekili olduğum ile bakmak istiyorum. Evet, geçen yıl biz 4 milletvekili olarak bir rapor sunduk ve bu rapor sonrasında bazı düzenlemeler oldu ama hâlâ devam eden birçok sorun var. Mesela, bir yıl içerisinde 8 -bu sadece kayıt altına alınan- şiddet
vakası var. Yine, iddiaya göre, Kızıltepe Devlet Hastanesinde milyonlara varan bir yolsuzluk durumu var ve Bakanlık müfettişleri oraya gittiler, bunu araştırdılar ama bu yolsuzluğun en çok da hastanenin yönetimiyle alakası olduğu iddia ediliyor. Yine, bu hastanenin idari ve mali müdürünün aynı zamanda SAĞLIK-SEN sendikasının da Başkanı olduğu iddia ediliyor. Biz buradan Bakanlığı değerlendirme için tarafsız bir teftişe davet ediyoruz.
Yine, Dargeçit'te, biliyorsunuz, orada da yine hastaneyle ilgili birçok sorun var. 25 yataklı yeni bir hastane yapılması söz konusu. Bir an önce bu hastanenin başlamasını talep ediyoruz ama var olan hastanede de yönetim, çalışanların taleplerini, sorunlarını dile getirmek için verdikleri dilekçeleri kayda almıyor ve çalışanlar eğer kayıt yoksa sorun da yoktur yaklaşımıyla karşı karşıya kalıyor. Zaten Mazıdağı'nda neredeyse hiç doktor yok. Yine, birçok branşta uzman doktor yok ve en çok da çocuk alanında uzman doktor yok; yan dalda, işte, nefrolojide, hematoloji de yine endokrin gibi yan dal çocuk alanında yan dalda uzman eksikliği var, ekipman eksikliği var, kreş ve sosyal destek sağlanmıyor, iş yükü çok fazla.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Teşekkür ediyoruz.
Selamlayın, mikrofonu açmıyoruz, biliyorsunuz.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Bunların hepsine çözüm bekliyoruz ve biz gerçekten sağlıkta dönüşüm yerine toplumsal bir sağlığı, kamu şehir hastanelerinin özel ortaklığından çıkarılmasını, sağlık çalışanlarının...
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Teşekkür ediyoruz.
Sayın İsmail Faruk Aksu Bey...
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Yine ana dilinde sağlık hizmetini...
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Aydeniz Hanım, teşekkür ediyorum.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - ...önceleyen bir sağlık sistemi olması için bütçeyi konuşmamız gerektiğini ifade ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Thank you, President.
I also greet everyone with respect.
First of all, today is the death anniversary of Uğur Kaymaz, who was murdered in Kızıltepe twenty-one years ago as a result of the war policies that we are fighting to end, I would like to start by respectfully commemorating him.
Yes, Mr. Minister has painted a very good picture in health, but when we even look at the Health Transformation Program, it becomes clear that this picture is not like this. Deepening corruption, institutional inadequacies in the Health Transformation Program, unqualified appointments, health status as much as money, millions transferred to private hospitals and private capital through city hospitals. Unfortunately, the systematic insecurity of labor, gangs in healthcare, cases of violence faced by healthcare workers and regional inequalities have unfortunately deepened. After all, it is not the patient, the citizen; tender, capitalist; In this system, companies, not healthcare workers, have priority. The jugular vein of the system is now clogged with rent, lack of control and insecurity. You have been talking about "transformation" and "reform" for twenty years, but unfortunately, patients have turned into customers, hospitals have turned into shopping malls, medicine has turned into performance, and healthcare workers have turned into subcontractors. Transformation in Health is an old shirt that was put on Türkiye in 2003 by the IMF and the World Bank and whose tags had fallen off, but unfortunately we did not fit into this shirt. It started with such a promise, with universal health insurance coverage, but faced the debt burden of millions of citizens. City hospitals were built outside the city. The rent paid to 5 hospitals has exceeded the investment budget of the Ministry of Health. Unfortunately, while these hospitals were opened, public hospitals were closed. Transportation became difficult, service decreased, workload increased, and corruption was legitimized with this system. Even infant and child deaths were indexed to the profits of companies. In response to death, the Ministry was satisfied with only fines.
Again, one of the biggest problems in health is the lack of health care in the mother tongue. There are millions of Kurds in Türkiye and their native language is Kurdish. Unfortunately, there is no service in native language. The ban on the language of millions of people caused communication problems between hundreds and millions of patients and healthcare professionals, and these caused many vital problems. We can give dozens or hundreds of examples of this. Of course, this is not just a communication problem, it is a direct medical error and a cause of death. Unfortunately, the health care standard in the native language of the World Health Organization does not even exist on paper in Türkiye. That's why health care in the mother tongue should be an indispensable service.
Again, I want to look at Mardin as its representative. Yes, we, as 4 members of parliament, submitted a report last year and after this report, some arrangements were made, but there are still many problems that continue. For example, in one year there were 8 incidents of violence - this is just recorded.
There is a case. Again, it is claimed that there is corruption in Kızıltepe State Hospital worth millions and Ministry inspectors went there and investigated it, but it is claimed that this corruption is mostly related to the management of the hospital.

Again, it is claimed that the administrative and financial manager of this hospital is also the President of the SAĞLIK-SEN union. We hereby invite the Ministry to an impartial inspection for evaluation.
Again, in Dargeçit, as you know, there are many problems with the hospital there too. There is a plan to build a new hospital with 25 beds. We demand that this hospital be started as soon as possible, but in the existing hospital, the management does not record the demands of the employees and the petitions they submit to express their problems, and the employees are faced with the approach that if there is no record, there is no problem. There are almost no doctors in Mazıdağı anyway. Again, there are no specialist doctors in many branches, and most of all there are no specialist doctors in the field of children; There is a lack of specialists in the sub-branch, work, nephrology, hematology and endocrine, as well as sub-specialists in the pediatric field, there is a lack of equipment, nursery and social support are not provided, the workload is too high.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Thank you.
Hello, we do not turn on the microphone, you know.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - We are waiting for a solution to all of these, and instead of a transformation in health, we really want a social health, removal of public city hospitals from private partnerships, health workers.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Thank you.
Dear Mr. İsmail Faruk Aksu.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Again, health services in their native language.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Ms. Aydeniz, thank you.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - I express that we need to talk about the budget in order to have a health system that prioritizes.

Milyonlarca kişinin dilinin yasak olması, yüzlerce, milyonlarca hasta ile sağlık çalışanları arasında iletişim sorunları yaşanmasına sebep oldu ve bunlar da birçok hayati meseleye sebep oldu. Bununla ilgili onlarca, yüzlerce örnek verebiliriz. Bu sadece bir iletişim sorunu değil tabii ki, doğrudan tıbbi bir hata ve ölüm nedenidir. Dünya Sağlık Örgütünün ana dilinde sağlık hizmeti standardı maalesef Türkiye'de kâğıt üzerinde bile yok. O yüzden ana dilinde sağlık hizmeti aslında olmazsa olmaz bir hizmet olmalı.
Yine, Mardin'e, vekili olduğum ile bakmak istiyorum. Evet, geçen yıl biz 4 milletvekili olarak bir rapor sunduk ve bu rapor sonrasında bazı düzenlemeler oldu ama hâlâ devam eden birçok sorun var. Mesela, bir yıl içerisinde 8 -bu sadece kayıt altına alınan- şiddet
vakası var. Yine, iddiaya göre, Kızıltepe Devlet Hastanesinde milyonlara varan bir yolsuzluk durumu var ve Bakanlık müfettişleri oraya gittiler, bunu araştırdılar ama bu yolsuzluğun en çok da hastanenin yönetimiyle alakası olduğu iddia ediliyor. Yine, bu hastanenin idari ve mali müdürünün aynı zamanda SAĞLIK-SEN sendikasının da Başkanı olduğu iddia ediliyor. Biz buradan Bakanlığı değerlendirme için tarafsız bir teftişe davet ediyoruz.
Yine, Dargeçit'te, biliyorsunuz, orada da yine hastaneyle ilgili birçok sorun var. 25 yataklı yeni bir hastane yapılması söz konusu. Bir an önce bu hastanenin başlamasını talep ediyoruz ama var olan hastanede de yönetim, çalışanların taleplerini, sorunlarını dile getirmek için verdikleri dilekçeleri kayda almıyor ve çalışanlar eğer kayıt yoksa sorun da yoktur yaklaşımıyla karşı karşıya kalıyor. Zaten Mazıdağı'nda neredeyse hiç doktor yok. Yine, birçok branşta uzman doktor yok ve en çok da çocuk alanında uzman doktor yok; yan dalda, işte, nefrolojide, hematoloji de yine endokrin gibi yan dal çocuk alanında yan dalda uzman eksikliği var, ekipman eksikliği var, kreş ve sosyal destek sağlanmıyor, iş yükü çok fazla.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Teşekkür ediyoruz.
Selamlayın, mikrofonu açmıyoruz, biliyorsunuz.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Bunların hepsine çözüm bekliyoruz ve biz gerçekten sağlıkta dönüşüm yerine toplumsal bir sağlığı, kamu şehir hastanelerinin özel ortaklığından çıkarılmasını, sağlık çalışanlarının...
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Teşekkür ediyoruz.
Sayın İsmail Faruk Aksu Bey...
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Yine ana dilinde sağlık hizmetini...
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Aydeniz Hanım, teşekkür ediyorum.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - ...önceleyen bir sağlık sistemi olması için bütçeyi konuşmamız gerektiğini ifade ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Thank you, President.
I also greet everyone with respect.
First of all, today is the death anniversary of Uğur Kaymaz, who was murdered in Kızıltepe twenty-one years ago as a result of the war policies that we are fighting to end, I would like to start by respectfully commemorating him.
Yes, Mr. Minister has painted a very good picture in health, but when we even look at the Health Transformation Program, it becomes clear that this picture is not like this. Deepening corruption, institutional inadequacies in the Health Transformation Program, unqualified appointments, health status as much as money, millions transferred to private hospitals and private capital through city hospitals. Unfortunately, the systematic insecurity of labor, gangs in healthcare, cases of violence faced by healthcare workers and regional inequalities have unfortunately deepened. After all, it is not the patient, the citizen; tender, capitalist; In this system, companies, not healthcare workers, have priority. The jugular vein of the system is now clogged with rent, lack of control and insecurity. You have been talking about "transformation" and "reform" for twenty years, but unfortunately, patients have turned into customers, hospitals have turned into shopping malls, medicine has turned into performance, and healthcare workers have turned into subcontractors. Transformation in Health is an old shirt that was put on Türkiye in 2003 by the IMF and the World Bank and whose tags had fallen off, but unfortunately we did not fit into this shirt. It started with such a promise, with universal health insurance coverage, but faced the debt burden of millions of citizens. City hospitals were built outside the city. The rent paid to 5 hospitals has exceeded the investment budget of the Ministry of Health. Unfortunately, while these hospitals were opened, public hospitals were closed. Transportation became difficult, service decreased, workload increased, and corruption was legitimized with this system. Even infant and child deaths were indexed to the profits of companies. In response to death, the Ministry was satisfied with only fines.
Again, one of the biggest problems in health is the lack of health care in the mother tongue. There are millions of Kurds in Türkiye and their native language is Kurdish. Unfortunately, there is no service in native language. The ban on the language of millions of people caused communication problems between hundreds and millions of patients and healthcare professionals, and these caused many vital problems. We can give dozens or hundreds of examples of this. Of course, this is not just a communication problem, it is a direct medical error and a cause of death. Unfortunately, the health care standard in the native language of the World Health Organization does not even exist on paper in Türkiye. That's why health care in the mother tongue should be an indispensable service.
Again, I want to look at Mardin as its representative. Yes, we, as 4 members of parliament, submitted a report last year and after this report, some arrangements were made, but there are still many problems that continue. For example, in one year there were 8 incidents of violence - this is just recorded.
There is a case. Again, it is claimed that there is corruption in Kızıltepe State Hospital worth millions and Ministry inspectors went there and investigated it, but it is claimed that this corruption is mostly related to the management of the hospital.

Again, it is claimed that the administrative and financial manager of this hospital is also the President of the SAĞLIK-SEN union. We hereby invite the Ministry to an impartial inspection for evaluation.
Again, in Dargeçit, as you know, there are many problems with the hospital there too. There is a plan to build a new hospital with 25 beds. We demand that this hospital be started as soon as possible, but in the existing hospital, the management does not record the demands of the employees and the petitions they submit to express their problems, and the employees are faced with the approach that if there is no record, there is no problem. There are almost no doctors in Mazıdağı anyway. Again, there are no specialist doctors in many branches, and most of all there are no specialist doctors in the field of children; There is a lack of specialists in the sub-branch, work, nephrology, hematology and endocrine, as well as sub-specialists in the pediatric field, there is a lack of equipment, nursery and social support are not provided, the workload is too high.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Thank you.
Hello, we do not turn on the microphone, you know.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - We are waiting for a solution to all of these, and instead of a transformation in health, we really want a social health, removal of public city hospitals from private partnerships, health workers.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Thank you.
Dear Mr. İsmail Faruk Aksu.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - Again, health services in their native language.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Ms. Aydeniz, thank you.
SALİHE AYDENİZ (Mardin) - I express that we need to talk about the budget in order to have a health system that prioritizes.
